blocks.content:
**k6 Script Authoring** is a **mode of Grafana Assistant** built for **Grafana Cloud k6** scripts. General Assistant answers questions across Grafana; this mode is tuned so answers look like **performance tests**: JavaScript you can run, with **checks** (response assertions), **thresholds** (pass or fail on metrics), **URL grouping** (stable series instead of one label per raw URL), realistic **stages** or executors, and optional **Tempo** or **Pyroscope** hooks.

**Why use it:** it removes the blank-page problem for API and service testing—especially for **QA** validating releases and **developers** who already know their routes but not k6 syntax. **Good fit** when you have Assistant enabled and can reach **Performance** in Grafana Cloud. **Poor fit** when Assistant is disabled, you cannot send traffic to any target environment, or you need a fully custom protocol test with no Assistant support—use manual scripting paths instead.

Refer to **Terms used in this path** on the path cover for definitions and links. Always **review Assistant output** before high-load or production-facing runs.

## Open k6 Script Authoring and pick a workflow

The first time you use the product UI in this path, sign in to **Grafana Cloud** if you have not already. Then complete the following steps:

1. On the main menu, click **Testing & synthetics** > **Performance**.
2. Click the **Assistant** icon at the top of the sidebar to open Grafana Assistant.
3. In the mode selector at the bottom of the Assistant panel, choose **k6 Script Authoring**.
4. When Assistant asks what you want to do, use the **Follow-up** buttons: **Write new test**, **Discover & generate**, or **Work with existing script**. The chat may label these as A, B, and C—pick the path that fits a new prompt, telemetry-backed discovery, or paste-and-improve.

   Expect outputs such as a full **JavaScript k6 script**, concrete **suggestions** (for example cardinality fixes or missing checks), or a **converted** script when you supplied Postman or curl—not generic Grafana answers unrelated to load testing.
In the next milestone, you generate a complete script from a plain-language prompt and learn how to read what Assistant produced.
